OPM.formula: Operating margin = Operating Profit ÷ Sales. Banks: Financing Margin %.

ROE.formula: Return on equity = Net Profit ÷ Net worth (Equity + Reserves).

ROA.formula: Return on assets = Net Profit ÷ Total Assets.

ROCE.formula: Return on capital employed — Screener's reported ROCE % (conceptually EBIT ÷ capital employed, capital employed = Total Assets − Current Liabilities); shown as reported, not re-derived here.

Operating leverage.formula: Operating leverage = Operating-profit growth ÷ Sales growth (YoY).

three_step.formula: ROE = Net margin × Asset turnover × Equity multiplier (= PAT/Sales × Sales/Assets × Assets/Networth).

five_step.formula: ROE = Tax burden × Interest burden × EBIT margin × Asset turnover × Leverage.

FCF.formula: Free Cash Flow = CFO − Capex (as reported by Screener).

CFO/PAT.formula: Cash conversion = CFO ÷ Net Profit (≥1 over time = profits are real cash).

FCF/PAT.formula: Free cash conversion = Free Cash Flow ÷ Net Profit.

Accrual ratio.formula: Accrual ratio = (Net Profit − CFO) ÷ Total Assets (Sloan; high = low quality).

Net worth.formula: Net worth = Equity Capital + Reserves (shareholders' equity).

Debt.formula: Total borrowings (balance sheet). Banks: borrowings + deposits.

Debt/EBITDA.formula: Gross Debt ÷ EBITDA (Operating Profit). Net debt not available (cash not isolated).

formulas.fcf_yield: FCF yield = Free Cash Flow ÷ Market cap.
formulas.ev_ebitda: EV/EBITDA = (Market cap + Debt) ÷ Operating Profit. Cash not netted (approx).

components.label: Profitability — median ROCE/ROE
components.note: Return on capital; higher = better business economics. Score: 10%→0, 25%→100.

components.label: Margin stability — CV
components.note: Coefficient of variation (std÷mean) of margin; lower = steadier. Score: 0.10→100, 0.50→0.

components.label: Growth consistency — % up years
components.note: Share of years with positive sales growth. Score: 50%→0, 100%→100.

components.label: Cash conversion — median CFO/PAT
components.note: Median CFO ÷ PAT; ≥1 means profits convert to cash. Score: 0.5→0, 1.0→100.

quality.method: Composite = equal-weighted average of the component scores below; every component shows its raw value and 0-100 mapping (house thresholds, adjustable). Not a black box.

cycle.method: Each gauge = the latest value's percentile and z-score within the company's OWN history (self-referenced cycle position; no peer data needed). Flags are signposts.

formulas.sales: Net sales / revenue (P&L). Banks: total revenue (interest + other income).
formulas.ebitda: Operating Profit = Sales − Operating Expenses (before depreciation, interest, tax).
formulas.eps: Earnings per share (₹) reported by Screener.
formulas.networth: Net worth = Equity Capital + Reserves (shareholders' equity).
formulas.bookvalue: Book value per share = Net worth ÷ shares; shares = Net Profit ÷ EPS.
formulas.debt: Total borrowings (balance sheet). Banks: borrowings + deposits.
formulas.fcf: Free Cash Flow = CFO − Capex (as reported by Screener).
formulas.opm: Operating margin = Operating Profit ÷ Sales. Banks: Financing Margin %.
formulas.roe: Return on equity = Net Profit ÷ Net worth (Equity + Reserves).
formulas.roa: Return on assets = Net Profit ÷ Total Assets.
formulas.roce: Return on capital employed — Screener's reported ROCE % (conceptually EBIT ÷ capital employed, capital employed = Total Assets − Current Liabilities); shown as reported, not re-derived here.
formulas.op_leverage: Operating leverage = Operating-profit growth ÷ Sales growth (YoY).
formulas.debt_ebitda: Gross Debt ÷ EBITDA (Operating Profit). Net debt not available (cash not isolated).
formulas.cfo_pat: Cash conversion = CFO ÷ Net Profit (≥1 over time = profits are real cash).
formulas.fcf_pat: Free cash conversion = Free Cash Flow ÷ Net Profit.
formulas.accrual_ratio: Accrual ratio = (Net Profit − CFO) ÷ Total Assets (Sloan; high = low quality).
formulas.dupont: ROE = Net margin × Asset turnover × Equity multiplier (= PAT/Sales × Sales/Assets × Assets/Networth).
formulas.dupont5: ROE = Tax burden × Interest burden × EBIT margin × Asset turnover × Leverage.
formulas.fcf_yield: FCF yield = Free Cash Flow ÷ Market cap.
formulas.ev_ebitda: EV/EBITDA = (Market cap + Debt) ÷ Operating Profit. Cash not netted (approx).

components.label: Preferred earnings (EPS)
components.note: revisions to the main earnings line

components.label: Secondary earnings (EBITDA)
components.note: revisions to the secondary earnings line

starmine.read: 97/100 — very strong upward revision momentum, and rising over the last month.
starmine.usage: Short-horizon (~1-month) analyst-revision momentum, region-relative percentile. It mean-reverts — read direction, not just level — and is best paired with valuation (high ARM + cheap = constructive). Not a valuation or buy-and-hold signal.
